hostile.transparentRgb: fully transparent green RGB remains observable between opaque bounds after chroma and crop

rejected: Crop-first implementation: the keyed pixels disappear before bounds are measured; operations and x=1 bounds prove chroma precedes crop.
Non-deterministic repeat: two real Worker runs produce identical pixels and operation order.
No-match over-removal: tolerance 0 with non-key colors keeps source RGB/alpha and full bounds.
Extreme tolerance crash/halo: opaque, semitransparent and transparent inputs yield explicit empty-output policy, bounded 1x1 surface and warning.
Transparent RGB loss: a transparent keyed-green pixel retains its RGB through chroma and is only removed by crop bounds policy.
adversarialAutopsy.boundary: The worker gate covers processing order and hostile chroma/crop outputs. UI process/results/export journeys remain G6/G7 work.
